generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance InternalTemperatureSensor1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Digital temperature sensor measuring internal temperature of the styrofoam box with ±0.5°C accuracy using 1-Wire protocol. Part of the critical sensor network for closed-loop temperature regulation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureDataStream1
]

instance InternalTemperatureSensor2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Digital temperature sensor measuring internal temperature of the styrofoam box with ±0.5°C accuracy using 1-Wire protocol. Provides redundant measurement for fault tolerance in temperature regulation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTemperatureDataStream2
]

instance ExternalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Digital temperature sensor measuring external ambient temperature with ±0.5°C accuracy using 1-Wire protocol. Provides environmental context for thermal model calibration."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ExternalTemperatureDataStream
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance InternalTemperatureDataStream1 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor1
    base:desc "Continuous or periodic temperature data stream from internal sensor 1 (likely sampled every few seconds) for thermal regulation and model calibration."
]

instance InternalTemperatureDataStream2 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor2
    base:desc "Continuous or periodic temperature data stream from internal sensor 2 (likely sampled every few seconds) for thermal regulation and model calibration."
]

instance ExternalTemperatureDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ExternalTemperatureSensor
    base:desc "Continuous or periodic ambient temperature data stream for environmental context in thermal model calibration."
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance TwoParameterThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simplified thermal dynamics model with two free parameters representing heat transfer characteristics. Used for initial temperature prediction and calibration baseline."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler
]

instance FourParameterThermalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "High-fidelity thermal dynamics model with four free parameters capturing detailed thermal inertia. Demonstrates superior accuracy in predicting thermal behavior."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler, PredictiveSimulationEnabler
]

instance RawSensorTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Unprocessed time-series temperature data from all three sensors (internal and external) collected at runtime."
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTemperatureDataStream1, InternalTemperatureDataStream2, ExternalTemperatureDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreprocessingEnabler
]

instance ProcessedSensorData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Filtered and normalized sensor data reconciled with model predictions through calibration algorithms. Used for real-time monitoring and validation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData RawSensorTemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler
]

instance CalibratedThermalParameters : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Refined thermal model parameters (2-4 parameters) adjusted through calibration algorithms to minimize prediction errors against observed sensor data."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ProcessedSensorData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PredictiveSimulationEnabler, ControlOptimizationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance DataPreprocessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python-based preprocessing module using SciPy for filtering, normalization, and initial data validation of raw sensor streams."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, FaultDetectionService
]

instance ThermalModelCalibrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Calibration algorithm implemented in Python using SciPy to adjust model parameters (2-4 parameters) via least-squares optimization against observed sensor data."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveSimulationService, ModelValidationService
]

instance PredictiveSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Numerical solver for differential equations representing thermal dynamics, executed in Python using SciPy to simulate system behavior under current conditions."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveSimulationService
]

instance ControlOptimizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Optimization algorithm for adjusting heatbed power and fan speed to achieve energy-efficient temperature regulation while maintaining safety thresholds."
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlAdjustmentService
]

instance RabbitMQCommunicationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Message broker for inter-process communication between Raspberry Pi controller and physical actuators (heatbed). Ensures reliable transmission of actuation commands."
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlAdjustmentService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous evaluation of PT performance using processed sensor data to detect temperature deviations and operational anomalies."
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureDeviationInsight, SystemStateInsight
]

instance PredictiveSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Execution of calibrated thermal models to predict PT behavior under current or hypothetical operating conditions."
    DTDFVocab:provides ThermalBehaviorPredictionInsight, FutureStateProjectionInsight
]

instance FaultDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Integration of anomaly detection algorithms to identify sensor malfunctions, thermal drift, or control system failures."
    DTDFVocab:provides FaultDetectionInsight
]

instance ControlAdjustmentService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment of heatbed power and fan speed based on optimization recommendations to maintain target temperature."
    DTDFVocab:provides ActuationCommandAction, EnergyOptimizationAction
]

instance ModelValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Periodic validation of model accuracy by comparing predictions with real-time sensor data to ensure calibration integrity."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelAccuracyInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance TemperatureDeviationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time identification of temperature deviations from target setpoints, including magnitude and duration."
]

instance SystemStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Current operational state of the incubator including internal/ambient temperature, heatbed status, and fan operation."
]

instance ThermalBehaviorPredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted thermal behavior of the incubator under current conditions, including transient responses and steady-state predictions."
]

instance FutureStateProjectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Projected system state under hypothetical conditions (e.g., external temperature changes, content variations) for proactive decision-making."
]

instance FaultDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identified anomalies including sensor failures, thermal drift, or control system malfunctions with confidence levels."
]

instance ModelAccuracyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantitative assessment of model prediction errors against observed sensor data, including parameter confidence intervals."
]

instance ActuationCommandAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated command to adjust heatbed power or fan speed based on control optimization recommendations."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EnergyOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Recommendation to adjust control parameters for improved energy efficiency while maintaining temperature regulation."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance HeatbedActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Primary thermal actuator controlled via Raspberry Pi to maintain internal temperature through resistive heating. Operates within predefined power constraints."
]

instance FanActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Mechanical actuator ensuring uniform temperature distribution within the styrofoam box through forced convection."
]

// C1: Physical Components
instance StyrofoamInsulatedBox : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Insulated container minimizing thermal losses through low-conductivity styrofoam material. Provides thermal inertia for stable temperature regulation."
]

instance RaspberryPiController : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Embedded controller interfacing with sensors and actuators, running Python-based control algorithms and communication protocols."
]

instance IncubatorEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Operational environment including internal incubator space and external ambient conditions affecting thermal dynamics."
    base:contains StyrofoamInsulatedBox, HeatbedActuator, FanActuator, InternalTemperatureSensor1, InternalTemperatureSensor2, ExternalTemperatureSensor
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin exerts control over the Physical Twin (PT) via **command signals** generated by the cyber component (Raspberry Pi), which adjusts the **heatbed** and **fan** to regulate the internal temperature of the incubator. The **command types** include activation/deactivation of the heatbed and modulation of the fan speed, though exact control message formats (e.g., PWM signals, binary on/off) are unspecified. **Validation and authentication mechanisms** are not explicitly described, but the system’s closed-loop nature implies implicit validation through feedback from temperature sensors. **Feedback systems** are critical: the DT continuously compares predicted vs. actual temperatures (via sensor data) to detect deviations, triggering corrective actions (e.g., adjusting heatbed power or fan speed). **Conflict resolution** is not addressed, but the use of a single controller (Raspberry Pi) suggests centralized arbitration. **Command execution confirmation** is inferred through the closed-loop monitoring process, where sensor data validates whether the PT responded as expected to digital commands."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system for the incubator relies on **real-time sensor data collection** from the Physical Twin (PT), where temperature sensors (two inside the insulated container and one outside) provide continuous input to the cyber-physical system. The **synchronization frequency** is implicitly tied to the **sampling rate of the sensors**, which is not explicitly quantified in the documents but is inferred to be **periodic and sufficiently frequent** to enable accurate model calibration and monitoring. The system employs **calibration algorithms** to update the models (e.g., differential equation-based thermal models) with the best estimate of parameters, suggesting a **closed-loop feedback mechanism** where sensor data is processed in near-real-time. The trade-off between **model accuracy and computational speed** is acknowledged, as the documents note that 'accurate models are also very slow to analyse,' implying that the temporal granularity of simulations may vary depending on the abstraction level of the model (e.g., a model with **two free parameters** vs. one with **four free parameters**). The **latency requirements** are not explicitly defined, but the system’s reliance on a **Raspberry Pi** for control and a **RabbitMQ server** for synchronization suggests a **low-latency communication framework** to ensure timely updates between the PT and DT components. The **temporal granularity** for monitoring and control is likely aligned with the **thermodynamic response time** of the incubator, which may range from **seconds to minutes** depending on the thermal inertia of the system and the desired precision of the temperature regulation."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin constellation for the incubator follows a **cyber-physical integration pattern**, where the DT orchestrates interactions between the **Physical Twin (PT)**—comprising the insulated container, heatbed, fan, and sensors—and the **Cyber Twin (CT)**—implemented via a Raspberry Pi controller and simulation models. The architecture employs a **closed-loop control system** with the following key components:
  1. **Data Collection Layer**: Sensors (two internal, one external temperature sensors) stream real-time data to the DT.
  2. **Model Layer**: Two thermal models (2-parameter and 4-parameter) are calibrated using **parameter estimation algorithms** to align with PT behavior.
  3. **Algorithm Layer**: Calibration algorithms update model parameters, while **monitoring algorithms** (e.g., fault detectors) assess system performance. The **Raspberry Pi** acts as the central orchestrator, executing control logic and coordinating data flow.
  The system leverages **feedback loops** for dynamic adaptation, where deviations between PT and DT states trigger recalibration or corrective actions. Resource management is implicit, with computational demands distributed between the **model simulation** (potentially offloaded to higher-performance systems) and the **real-time controller** (handled by the Raspberry Pi). The constellation lacks explicit mention of **distributed coordination protocols** (e.g., consensus algorithms) but relies on **centralized control** with modular components."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle for the described incubator case study includes the following phases: **1. System Design and Modeling**: The DT is built by integrating models of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and its environment, including both cyber (e.g., control algorithms) and physical components (e.g., temperature sensors, heatbed, fan). Models are developed at varying levels of abstraction, with calibration algorithms used to refine model parameters (e.g., thermal dynamics) based on real-time sensor data from the Physical Twin (PT). **2. Deployment and Integration**: The DT is deployed alongside the PT, where it continuously receives real-time data from sensors (e.g., two internal temperature sensors and one external sensor) to update its models. **3. Monitoring and Adaptation**: The DT employs monitors to check system performance (e.g., temperature regulation safety and optimality) and triggers corrective actions (e.g., via human intervention or automated controllers) when deviations are detected. **4. Evolution and Maintenance**: The DT evolves through iterative calibration and model refinement, leveraging feedback loops from the PT to adapt to changing environmental conditions (e.g., external temperature fluctuations). Governance is implicit through the use of calibration algorithms and supervisory controllers to ensure alignment between the DT and PT. The lifecycle emphasizes **runtime model adaptation** and **closed-loop optimization** to maintain system robustness and predictability."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) emphasizes **model fidelity** and **validity** through structured **validation and verification (V&V) processes**. In the incubator case study, the **4-parameter thermal model** was empirically validated against the **2-parameter model**, demonstrating superior fidelity in predicting thermal inertia—a critical aspect of the PT’s behavior. **Uncertainty sources** include model parameter inaccuracies, environmental disturbances (e.g., ambient temperature fluctuations), and sensor noise, which are quantified through comparative experiments. The DT framework employs **calibration algorithms** to iteratively refine model parameters using real-time sensor data, reducing uncertainty and improving predictive accuracy. **Error detection and correction mechanisms** are implicit in the DT’s monitoring loop: deviations between simulated and observed PT behavior trigger alerts or automated adjustments (e.g., via supervisory controllers). **Quality assurance** is ensured by continuous model-data alignment, with human or algorithmic interventions addressing violations (e.g., unsafe or suboptimal PT performance). The broader DT context (Document 1) underscores the need for **rigorous V&V methods**, including differential equation-based dynamic modeling and empirical testing, to maintain model validity across operational conditions."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) infrastructure relies on a **Cyber-Physical System (CPS)** architecture, where the Raspberry Pi serves as the central node connecting the cyber (computational) and physical (sensor/actuator) components. The architecture prioritizes real-time interaction between the DT and PT."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin is hosted on a **Raspberry Pi** (edge deployment), which integrates the cyber components (controller, simulation models, calibration algorithms) and interfaces directly with the physical system. **Computational requirements** are modest, as the models are described as having **two or four free parameters** (suggesting lightweight differential equations or lookup tables for thermal dynamics). **Storage requirements** are minimal, focusing on sensor data logs and model parameters. **Scalability mechanisms** are not addressed, but the modular design (e.g., separate models for different abstraction levels) implies potential for extension. **Deployment models** are limited to on-premise/edge due to the hardware constraints (Raspberry Pi), with no mention of cloud integration. **Availability/reliability assurance** relies on the Raspberry Pi’s continuous operation and the redundancy of internal temperature sensors, though no explicit failover or backup systems are described."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system for the incubator case study incorporates **safety-critical considerations** through its integration of **monitoring algorithms** that check for safe and optimal system performance. The DT employs **calibration algorithms** to update models of the Cyber-Physical System (CPS) and environment, ensuring accurate parameter estimation. **Fail-safe mechanisms** are implied via the DT’s ability to detect violations in system behavior, triggering human or algorithmic interventions to modify the physical twin (PT) when necessary. While the documents do not explicitly detail **cybersecurity protocols** (e.g., encryption, access control, or authentication systems), the DT framework inherently relies on **secure data transmission** from sensors (e.g., temperature sensors) to the DT for real-time monitoring and control. The **risk assessment** is embedded in the DT’s supervisory role, where deviations from expected behavior are flagged for corrective action. No explicit mention of **data anonymization**, **consent management**, or **data sharing agreements** is provided in the context documents."
